tenant_id.description: Copied from authenticated TenantContext; never from untrusted message payload

payload.description: Typed redacted event-specific payload without credentials or private reasoning

info.description: Design-only at-least-once contracts for state-owner coordination.
info.title: FaultWitness Command and Event Design

commands.purpose: Create or return an idempotent tenant-scoped Incident.
Record a valid Runtime Task attempt starting investigation.
Record an evidence-linked diagnosis and Final Report.
Record a canonical ActionProposal and its immutable digest.
Record approve reject or expiry against the exact pending digest.
Project a typed known or uncertain action result into Incident state.
Record postcondition evidence and select resolve compensate or escalate.
Record verified compensation or an uncertain rollback outcome.
Request tenant-scoped Incident cancellation at a safe point.
Terminate an Incident with a typed reason and retained evidence.
Lease a pending task with a new attempt and monotonic fencing token.
Start work under the current task lease and fencing token.
Commit a terminal task result from a valid fenced attempt.
Pause after durable graph interrupt checkpoint and release the lease.
Requeue a paused task as a new attempt from its checkpoint.
Schedule bounded backoff or requeue while attempt budget remains.
Record a typed non-retryable or exhausted terminal task failure.
Request and complete task cancellation after a safe checkpoint.
Apply a typed guarded graph transition and durable checkpoint.
Create bounded novel probes when evidence remains insufficient.
Create a typed evidence-linked ActionProposal without executing it.
Request risk scope and obligation evaluation for a canonical proposal.
Resume an interrupted graph with a matching immutable approval grant.
Submit an authorized proposal to the sole privileged executor.
Request typed compensation for a verified failed postcondition.
Terminate the graph with a typed safe escalation reason.
Record R1 authorization R2 approval requirement or R3 rejection.
Apply approve reject expiry or cancel to the pending ActionTransaction.
Dispatch one typed authorized action after all bindings revalidate.
Record a known receipt or explicit uncertain effect without blind retry.
Evaluate versioned external postconditions and persist evidence.
Execute and verify the typed compensation bound to the digest.
Read external truth to resolve uncertainty without redispatch.
Cancel an action only before dispatch and invalidate its approval.

events.purpose: Announce durable tenant-scoped Incident creation.
Announce that a valid Runtime attempt started investigation.
Publish an evidence-linked diagnosis-only terminal result.
Publish an Incident action proposal and immutable digest.
Publish a tenant-scoped decision for an exact action digest.
Publish a known or uncertain ActionTransaction result.
Publish attributable Incident postcondition verification.
Request compensation for a verified failed outcome.
Publish verified or uncertain compensation outcome.
Announce cancellation intent pending a safe checkpoint.
Announce terminal Incident cancellation without uncertain effects.
Publish terminal escalation with typed reason and evidence.
Publish a new attempt lease and fencing token metadata.
Publish valid task attempt start.
Publish typed retry reason and bounded backoff.
Publish task return to pending for a new attempt.
Publish fenced terminal task success.
Publish durable paused checkpoint and lease release.
Publish authorized resume into a new attempt.
Publish exhausted retry terminal state and history.
Publish typed non-retryable terminal failure.
Publish task cancellation intent and token invalidation.
Publish safe terminal task cancellation.
Publish a versioned durable graph transition.
Publish typed ActionProposal creation without execution.
Publish a canonical policy decision request.
Publish versioned risk scope and obligations.
Publish an immutable R2 approval request.
Publish authorized request to the sole Action Executor.
Publish completed graph report and evidence lineage.
Publish typed graph escalation and remaining uncertainty.
Publish policy or approval authorization bound to a digest.
Publish terminal policy or approver rejection.
Publish terminal approval expiry.
Publish terminal pre-dispatch cancellation.
Publish attributable typed SUT dispatch.
Publish known typed external action receipt.
Publish explicit unknown external or compensation effect.
Publish verified successful action postconditions.
Publish typed compensation dispatch start.
Publish verified successful compensation.
Publish terminal requirement for manual reconciliation.
Publish read-only reconciliation evidence for prior uncertainty.

errors.meaning: Typed request or structured payload is invalid and must be corrected.
OIDC identity is missing invalid expired or unavailable.
Authenticated identity lacks tenant scope policy or data authority.
Tenant-scoped resource does not exist or is intentionally concealed.
Idempotency digest resource version or expected state version conflicts.
External effect is unknown and requires reconciliation rather than retry.
Tenant quota concurrency or backpressure currently rejects new work.
A required dependency is unavailable under its fixed degradation policy.
Worker fencing token or attempt no longer owns commit authority.
Attempt deadline or retry budget is exhausted and work cannot requeue.

failures.behavior: Reject before Incident creation and ignore all user supplied identity fields.
failures.detection: token signature audience expiry and tenant claim validation

failures.behavior: Return the original compatible Incident result without creating another task.
failures.detection: same tenant and Idempotency-Key reaches Control API

failures.behavior: Commit neither owned state nor transactional outbox and surface dependency failure.
failures.detection: transaction commit raises or loses database connection

failures.behavior: Retain the outbox row and publish until acknowledged while consumers deduplicate event_id.
failures.fault: Outbox publication fails after state commit
failures.state_effect: Owned state remains committed and event pending

failures.behavior: Reject stale commit and recover a new attempt from the last durable checkpoint.
failures.detection: fencing token no longer matches Scheduler state

failures.behavior: Apply bounded retry budget then degrade explicitly or escalate with the failure record.
failures.fault: Model or read tool returns timeout rate limit or transient 5xx
failures.state_effect: Graph continues degraded or reaches ESCALATE

failures.behavior: Attempt bounded repair and escalate when repair budget is exhausted.
failures.state_effect: Graph remains bounded or reaches ESCALATE

failures.behavior: Mark knowledge missing and block any write whose safety depends on that context.
failures.detection: Knowledge Service dependency health and typed query failure
failures.state_effect: Read degrades and action path fails closed

failures.behavior: Pause large-result flow and never substitute an unbounded payload in Agent context.
failures.detection: Artifact write or read returns typed dependency failure

failures.behavior: Buffer bounded sanitized events allow qualified reads only fail writes closed and forbid Gate closure.
failures.detection: instrumentation export and health confirmation fail
failures.fault: Authoritative Trace or Eval service is unavailable
failures.state_effect: Write path blocked and Eval remains incomplete

failures.behavior: Deny all write actions without a fallback allow decision.
failures.detection: policy request timeout or health failure
failures.state_effect: Action stays PREPARED or graph ESCALATE

failures.behavior: Reject new requests and approvals while an existing read-only run may finish on a valid snapshot.
failures.detection: token validation or identity health cannot complete
failures.state_effect: No new state or approval commit

failures.behavior: Enter UNCERTAIN and reconcile external truth before any further action.
failures.detection: adapter timeout after dispatch without attributable receipt
failures.fault: Action may execute but its response is lost

failures.behavior: Run defined compensation or enter MANUAL and escalate when not compensable.
failures.fault: Typed action completes but postconditions fail

failures.behavior: Preserve UNCERTAIN then require manual reconciliation without blind retry.
Apply at a safe checkpoint and verify or escalate any possible external side effect.
failures.fault: Cancellation arrives before or during work
failures.state_effect: Cancel request then CANCELLED or ESCALATED

failures.behavior: Deny access emit security audit and invalidate contaminated evaluation or training runs.
failures.detection: sealed identity storage and network policy denial
failures.fault: Agent Runtime or developer requests locked Ground Truth
failures.state_effect: No Agent state receives protected data

Conflict.description: Idempotency digest or state version conflict

info.description: Frozen public contract implemented by the I-0012 Control API runtime.
info.title: FaultWitness Control API Design

post.summary: Create or return an idempotent tenant-scoped Incident

get.summary: Get the current tenant-scoped Incident snapshot

post.summary: Approve or reject the exact pending action digest
Request cancellation at the next safe checkpoint

200.description: Server-sent event stream; retention gaps terminate with an explicit typed event

get.summary: Stream truthful typed events with monotonic resume IDs

post.summary: Append user feedback without rewriting terminal state

get.summary: List the authenticated tenant and role scoped Skill catalog
List the authenticated tenant and role scoped tool catalog

invariants: Action Digest binds tenant environment action parameters resource version preconditions postconditions compensation risk approver expiry and single-use token.
Action Executor is the sole component with SUT write credentials and arbitrary shell or Kubernetes writes are prohibited.
COMMITTED requires successful postcondition evidence and R2 dispatch requires a matching immutable approval.
UNCERTAIN has no automatic outgoing transition and permits reconciliation or manual handling only.

transitions.failure_semantics: Stale policy resource or digest blocks authorization.
transitions.guard: current policy authorizes R1 action

transitions.failure_semantics: No write credential or dispatch is available while approval is pending.
transitions.guard: current policy classifies action as R2

transitions.failure_semantics: A prohibited proposal terminates without side effect.
transitions.guard: policy classifies action as R3 or denies scope

transitions.failure_semantics: Any mismatch returns conflict and leaves the action unexecuted.
transitions.guard: approval matches immutable digest and current resource

transitions.failure_semantics: The rejected single-use digest cannot later authorize dispatch.
transitions.guard: authorized approver rejects proposal

transitions.failure_semantics: Expiry requires a new proposal digest and approval.
transitions.guard: approval validity window expires

transitions.failure_semantics: Cancellation invalidates any later approval for the digest.
transitions.guard: cancel arrives before approval and dispatch

transitions.failure_semantics: Tenant policy digest version or idempotency conflict prevents SUT invocation.
transitions.guard: all authorization bindings still match at dispatch

transitions.failure_semantics: A dispatch racing after cancellation is rejected by state version.
transitions.guard: cancel commits before dispatch

transitions.failure_semantics: Duplicate receipt returns the existing transaction result.
transitions.guard: adapter returns an attributable known receipt

transitions.failure_semantics: No automatic redispatch is allowed while external effect may exist.
transitions.guard: response lost timeout or conflicting receipt leaves effect unknown

transitions.failure_semantics: COMMITTED is impossible without attributable postcondition evidence.
transitions.guard: all versioned postconditions pass

transitions.failure_semantics: Failed verification cannot be hidden as successful commit.
transitions.guard: postconditions fail and compensation is defined

transitions.failure_semantics: Non-compensable failure preserves evidence for manual recovery.
transitions.guard: postcondition failure is non-compensable

transitions.failure_semantics: ROLLED_BACK requires verified compensation evidence.
transitions.guard: compensation postconditions pass

transitions.failure_semantics: Unknown compensation cannot be retried blindly or declared rolled back.
transitions.guard: compensation response or effect is unknown

transitions.failure_semantics: Manual handling retains the original and compensation evidence.
transitions.guard: compensation cannot execute safely

transitions.failure_semantics: Reconciliation reads external truth and never redispatches the action.
transitions.guard: read-only reconciliation establishes a known effect

transitions.failure_semantics: Unresolved uncertainty terminates in manual handling, not success or retry.
transitions.guard: reconciliation deadline expires without known effect

invariants: The graph emits ToolCall and ActionProposal only and has no shell Kubernetes or direct SUT write capability.
LLM decision nodes are separated from deterministic policy execution reducers and state commits.
Every important transition checkpoints step tool token cost deadline and no-progress budgets.
Private chain-of-thought is never persisted; public rationale is structured and evidence linked.

transitions.failure_semantics: Invalid intake terminates in escalation without invoking a model or tool.
transitions.guard: IncidentSpec and TenantContext validate

transitions.failure_semantics: Ambiguous or forbidden scope escalates rather than broadening access.
transitions.guard: service scope risk and mode are explicit

transitions.failure_semantics: Invalid structured plan gets bounded repair then escalation.
transitions.guard: typed bounded plan validates

transitions.failure_semantics: Missing knowledge is explicit and any dependent write is blocked.
transitions.guard: retrieval result is ACL filtered and provenance complete

transitions.failure_semantics: Collector errors remain typed evidence gaps and cannot be converted to facts.
transitions.guard: bounded collectors have returned or timed out

transitions.failure_semantics: Schema conflicts or duplicates are quarantined and never silently merged.
transitions.guard: deterministic reducer validates evidence records

transitions.failure_semantics: Unsupported hypotheses are rejected or marked uncertain before verification.
transitions.guard: bounded hypotheses cite supporting and contradicting EvidenceRef

transitions.failure_semantics: Repeated or non-informative probes consume no unbounded loop and trigger escalation.
transitions.guard: evidence insufficient and progress budget remains

transitions.failure_semantics: A forbidden or invalid probe is rejected and cannot widen service scope.
transitions.guard: typed ProbePlan validates against tool scope

transitions.failure_semantics: Unsupported critical claims prevent report finalization.
transitions.guard: diagnosis complete and no action is selected

transitions.failure_semantics: Diagnosis-only mode or weak evidence cannot create an executable proposal.
transitions.guard: actionable conclusion has sufficient evidence

transitions.failure_semantics: The graph terminates with evidence gaps and never fabricates certainty.
transitions.guard: cannot judge safely or budget deadline exhausted

transitions.failure_semantics: A malformed or untraceable report is repaired within budget or escalated.
transitions.guard: Final Report schema and EvidenceRef coverage pass

transitions.failure_semantics: A proposal cannot reach policy without canonical parameters and preconditions.
transitions.guard: ActionProposal schema and immutable digest validate

transitions.failure_semantics: OPA outage or stale policy decision blocks dispatch.
transitions.guard: current policy allows R0 or R1 action

transitions.failure_semantics: The graph checkpoints and interrupts before any non-idempotent effect.
transitions.guard: current policy requires R2 approval

transitions.failure_semantics: Policy denial and outage fail closed without an execution path.
transitions.guard: policy denies R3 or cannot decide

transitions.failure_semantics: Digest version tenant or expiry mismatch cannot resume dispatch.
transitions.guard: unexpired approval matches the current immutable digest

transitions.failure_semantics: A rejected or expired digest is terminal for the proposal.
transitions.guard: approval rejected expired or cancelled

transitions.failure_semantics: Worker emits a command only; it never receives or uses SUT write credentials.
transitions.guard: typed authorized action command accepted by Action Executor

transitions.failure_semantics: Duplicate results are deduplicated and conflicting receipts escalate.
transitions.guard: ActionResult has a known attributable effect

transitions.failure_semantics: The graph cannot blind-retry or treat uncertainty as success.
transitions.guard: ActionTransaction is UNCERTAIN or MANUAL

transitions.failure_semantics: Finalization is blocked if the external postcondition is absent.
transitions.guard: postconditions pass and report is complete

transitions.failure_semantics: The graph only requests compensation; Action Executor remains the sole writer.
transitions.guard: postconditions fail and typed compensation exists

transitions.failure_semantics: Unsafe outcome terminates the graph for manual handling.
transitions.guard: outcome is non-compensable or uncertain

transitions.failure_semantics: Compensation execution remains in Action Executor and duplicate requests are idempotent.
transitions.guard: compensation command accepted with matching digest

invariants: R2 execution requires an unexpired approval that matches tenant environment action digest and resource version.
RESOLVED always has a Final Report and attributable postcondition or diagnosis-only evidence.
UNCERTAIN or MANUAL ActionTransaction forces Incident escalation.
Terminal Incident states never transition in place; later feedback appends events.

transitions.failure_semantics: Invalid identity is rejected before persistence; duplicate key returns the original Incident.
transitions.guard: authenticated tenant and valid IncidentSpec

transitions.failure_semantics: A stale or cross-tenant task event is rejected without changing Incident state.
transitions.guard: runtime task has a valid active attempt

transitions.failure_semantics: Missing report or unsupported critical claim prevents resolution and requires more evidence or escalation.
transitions.guard: diagnosis complete and mode is diagnosis_only

transitions.failure_semantics: A missing or stale policy decision blocks execution and escalates safely.
transitions.guard: R1 action is currently allowed by policy

transitions.failure_semantics: The proposal is recorded but no write is dispatched before a matching approval.
transitions.guard: policy classifies proposal as R2

transitions.failure_semantics: The Incident retains evidence and a typed reason instead of guessing or bypassing policy.
transitions.guard: R3 forbidden or evidence budget deadline exhausted

transitions.failure_semantics: Any digest scope version or expiry mismatch returns conflict and does not dispatch.
transitions.guard: approval grant is valid and matches the current action digest

transitions.failure_semantics: The rejected digest cannot be reused and no ActionTransaction executes.
transitions.guard: approver rejects the immutable proposal

transitions.failure_semantics: Expiry closes the approval opportunity and requires a newly digested proposal.
transitions.guard: approval deadline expires

transitions.failure_semantics: Only an attributable receipt advances verification; duplicates return the prior result.
transitions.guard: ActionResult has a known external effect

transitions.failure_semantics: Uncertain side effects prohibit automatic retry and require reconciliation or manual handling.
transitions.guard: ActionTransaction is UNCERTAIN or MANUAL

transitions.failure_semantics: Resolution is rejected if evidence or postcondition proof is missing.
transitions.guard: postconditions pass and Final Report is complete

transitions.failure_semantics: A failed postcondition cannot be reported as resolved while compensation is pending.
transitions.guard: postconditions fail and compensation is defined

transitions.failure_semantics: Non-compensable or unknown outcome is preserved for manual reconciliation.
transitions.guard: failure is non-compensable or uncertain

transitions.failure_semantics: Rollback is not treated as complete until its postcondition is verified.
transitions.guard: compensation is verified and system is healthy

transitions.failure_semantics: Uncertain compensation remains visible and requires manual reconciliation.
transitions.guard: compensation fails or remains uncertain

transitions.failure_semantics: Cancellation is idempotent and cannot cross tenant scope.
transitions.guard: cancel requested before queue commit

transitions.failure_semantics: Scheduler cancellation is emitted transactionally with the Incident terminal state.
transitions.guard: cancel requested before task start

transitions.failure_semantics: Possible external effects force verification or escalation instead of cancellation.
transitions.guard: cancel reaches a safe checkpoint with no possible action effect

transitions.failure_semantics: The approval digest becomes unusable when cancellation commits.
transitions.guard: cancel invalidates pending approval before dispatch

transitions.failure_semantics: Cancellation records intent only; the action effect must reach a known safe point first.
transitions.guard: cancel requested after dispatch

invariants: Delivery is at least once and every retry uses a new attempt_id under the stable task_id.
Only the current fencing token can checkpoint complete fail pause or dispatch downstream work.
PAUSED tasks do not retain a worker lease.
No distributed exactly-once behavior is claimed.

transitions.failure_semantics: Quota or capacity denial leaves the task pending with explicit backpressure.
transitions.guard: quota capacity and deadline allow an attempt

transitions.failure_semantics: A stale token is fenced and cannot start or commit work.
transitions.guard: worker presents the current lease before start deadline

transitions.failure_semantics: The attempt is retained and a new fencing token is required for retry.
transitions.guard: lease expires before worker start

transitions.failure_semantics: Lease loss or version conflict rejects the stale completion without overwriting state.
transitions.guard: worker commits a valid terminal result

transitions.failure_semantics: A task never pauses before durable checkpoint and releases its worker lease.
transitions.guard: graph interrupt has a committed checkpoint

transitions.failure_semantics: Resume creates a new attempt and fencing token instead of reviving an old lease.
transitions.guard: resume command is authorized and deadline remains

transitions.failure_semantics: Partial work is recovered only from committed checkpoints under a new attempt.
transitions.guard: retryable failure or lease loss is recorded

transitions.failure_semantics: Retries stop when attempt or deadline budget is exhausted.
transitions.guard: bounded attempts remain and backoff elapsed

transitions.failure_semantics: Exhausted work remains inspectable with the last checkpoint and failure history.
transitions.guard: attempt budget or deadline exhausted

transitions.failure_semantics: An untyped or stale failure cannot terminate the task.
transitions.guard: failure is typed non-retryable

transitions.failure_semantics: Cancellation is idempotent and retained until a safe terminal acknowledgement.
transitions.guard: authorized cancellation requested

transitions.failure_semantics: The fencing token is invalidated before cancellation completes.
transitions.guard: authorized cancellation invalidates current lease

transitions.failure_semantics: Worker checkpoints and stops at a safe point; action uncertainty is handled separately.
transitions.guard: authorized cancellation reaches running attempt

transitions.failure_semantics: The retained checkpoint remains auditable after cancellation.
transitions.guard: authorized cancellation reaches paused task

transitions.failure_semantics: No further attempt is enqueued after cancellation commits.
transitions.guard: authorized cancellation arrives during backoff

transitions.failure_semantics: A valid active worker or uncertain side effect blocks terminal cancellation.
transitions.guard: worker stopped or no active lease remains

invariants: Identity fields are never accepted from request body query or model output.
types.name: TenantContext

invariants: Service scope and budget are bounded before scheduling.
types.name: IncidentSpec

invariants: Only Control API mutates state and terminal states are immutable.
types.name: IncidentState

invariants: Stable task_id spans attempts and never authorizes another tenant.
types.name: RunTask

invariants: Every terminal failure has a typed reason and retained attempt history.
types.name: RunState

invariants: Fencing tokens are monotonic and stale workers cannot commit.
types.name: Lease

invariants: A change is evidence not causation and retains source version.
types.name: ChangeEvent

invariants: State is typed versioned bounded and excludes private chain-of-thought.
types.name: AgentState

invariants: Every supported critical claim resolves to immutable source and artifact versions.
types.name: EvidenceRef

invariants: Confidence never replaces cited supporting and contradicting evidence.
types.name: Hypothesis

invariants: Probe tools stay inside Incident scope and no-progress budget.
types.name: ProbePlan

invariants: Definitions expose no arbitrary shell and declare timeout error and authorization semantics.
types.name: ToolDefinition

invariants: Gateway revalidates schema tenant scope policy timeout and budget.
types.name: ToolCall

invariants: Results are typed sanitized bounded and treated as untrusted data.
types.name: ToolResult

invariants: Skill boundaries cannot widen authenticated tool or action authority.
types.name: SkillManifest

invariants: Proposal is not executable authority and digest binds every safety field.
types.name: ActionProposal

invariants: Grant is immutable single use and invalid after any bound field changes.
types.name: ApprovalGrant

invariants: Only Action Executor mutates the transaction and UNCERTAIN never blind retries.
types.name: ActionTransaction

invariants: Lineage and split isolation are preserved without private reasoning.
types.name: TrajectoryIR

invariants: Result is attributable to immutable inputs evaluator and candidate revision.
types.name: EvalResult

invariants: Event payload is typed redacted at-least-once and deduplicated by event_id.
types.name: DomainEvent
